ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO MEET ITS INITIAL BURDEN TO ESTABLISH THE PROBABLE VALIDITY OF THE CLAIM ON WHICH THE ATTACHMENT IS BASED. CCP 484.090. IN THIS ACTION THE ATTACHMENT MAY ONLY BE BASED ON RELIEF AGAINST TRANSFER (CC 3439.07) FOR WHICH NO EVIDENTIARY SHOWING OR LEGAL ARGUMENT IS MADE IN THE MOVING PAPERS (POINTS AND AUTHORITIES 5:1-6:12). Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order
Off Calendar Apr-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ST. PAUL MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER.

Appearances: Craig C. Daniel of Gluck Daniel LLP (415.510.2509) for defendant Poppi Metaxas; Kevin Dolan of Solan Park (415.777.3000) for plaintiff and moving party; Brent D. Meyer of the Meyer Law Group LLP (415.765.1588) for defendants Jerry Metaxas, 1415 Pacheco, Ionian Venture Partners, LLC.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party failed to meet its initial burden to establish the probable validity of the claim on which the attachment is based. CCP 484.090. In this action the attachment may only be based on relief against transfer (CC 3439.07) for which no evidentiary showing or legal argument is made in the moving papers (Points and Authorities 5:1-6:12). Order signed after hearing. Judge: Anne Christine Massullo, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/ACM)
